# `btpc config`

<!-- Generated by `scripts/generate-cli-reference.sh`; do not edit. -->

Locate, inspect, validate, and update configuration

**Parent:** [`btpc`](index.md)

See [Configuration and global flags](../configuration.md) and [Exit codes and streams](../index.md#exit-codes-and-streams).

## Synopsis

```text
Usage: btpc config [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>
```

## Subcommands

| Command | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`btpc config path`](config-path.md) | Print the selected configuration path |

| [`btpc config init`](config-init.md) | Create a minimal configuration file |

| [`btpc config show`](config-show.md) | Print configuration with secrets redacted by default |

| [`btpc config check`](config-check.md) | Validate schema, references, cycles, conflicts, and permissions |

| [`btpc config explain`](config-explain.md) | Explain resolved command values without executing the command |

| [`btpc config tracker`](config-tracker.md) | Manage named tracker aliases |

| [`btpc config preset`](config-preset.md) | Manage named creation presets |


## Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `-h, --help` | Print help |

## Global options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--config, <PATH>` | Use this configuration file when configuration loading is enabled |
| `--no-config` | Disable implicit and environment-selected configuration Default: `false`. |
| `--color, <COLOR>` | Control colored terminal output Values: `auto`, `always`, `never`. |
| `-v, --verbose` | Increase diagnostic verbosity; may be repeated Default: `0`. |
| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress human summaries, warnings, and progress Default: `false`. |

## Example

```console
$ btpc config --help
```
